channel.title: Shroud of the Avatar on Chasing Dings!
channel.description: Recent content in Shroud of the Avatar on Chasing Dings!

item.title: MMOs on the Bubble
item.description:
I don’t play every MMO that comes out – not by a longshot. But, I follow a lot of gamers, streamers, and game developers, and between me and them, I see a lot of discussion about the new MMOs that come down the pike.

Crowfall was released a couple of weeks ago. Suddenly, everyone was talking about it. And then, suddenly everyone was not. What did it all mean? I asked Google Trends to help me analyze searches for a dozen or so MMOs to help understand what all was going on with MMOs today.

item.title: Shroud of the Avatar: Choosing Fate
item.description: Peaked into Shroud of the Avatar again yesterday. This is Richard “Lord British” Garriott’s MMO-in-development that brings the morality-based gameplay of his Ultima RPG into a more modern incarnation. I backed this on Kickstarter about two and a half years ago, and have been checking into the alpha and beta releases on a regular basis since they started becoming available. Not EVERY one… but often enough.

item.title: Traipsing about in Shroud of the Avatar.
item.description:
Traipsing about in Shroud of the Avatar. It’s working. I didn’t see any other players, but every available housing plot I came across was all bought up. Ye shall know them by their claims….

This is definitely an explorer’s game. Though, I did like their “Deck”-based combat – instead of having all your skills available (which is an option), you can opt to have your skills randomly chosen from a deck of skills that is automatically made or made by you. That was pretty cool.
